Requirement Description: The system shall allow users to select their preferred currency from a dropdown menu located in the header/footer area of the public store for localized pricing. nopCommerce supports multi-currency configuration (Configuration → Currencies), where the store administrator defines available currencies with exchange rates. When a customer selects a different currency, all product prices, shipping costs, taxes, and order totals are automatically recalculated and displayed in the chosen currency using the configured exchange rate. The primary store currency is used internally for all transactions, and the selected display currency is applied for presentation purposes only. Currency selection persists across the user session and is stored in a cookie. The dropdown shall display currency names or symbols (e.g., US Dollar, Euro) and the system shall support international currency formats.

The system shall provide comprehensive user authentication functionality including: (1) Registration — a registration form with fields for gender, first name, last name, date of birth, email, company name (optional), newsletter subscription opt-in, and password with confirmation. Password strength validation enforces configurable minimum length, uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character requirements. Registration methods include standard account creation, email validation required, administrator approval required, or registration disabled. (2) Login — secure login with email/username and password, 'Remember Me' checkbox, and configurable maximum login failure lockout. (3) Account Management — customers can update personal information, change passwords, manage addresses, view order history, downloadable products, back-in-stock subscriptions, reward points, and return requests from a 'My Account' page. The system supports external authentication methods and CAPTCHA for anti-bot protection.

The system shall allow logged-in users to add items to a wishlist for future reference or deferred purchasing. A wishlist is a list of products that a customer can save and share with friends or transfer to the shopping cart later. When a product with variants is added, all chosen variants are included. If a customer adds the same product multiple times, the product appears once with an updated quantity. The wishlist page displays product details including SKU, image, name, price, quantity (editable), and total. Users can: remove items, update quantities via 'Update wishlist' button, move items to the shopping cart via 'Add to cart' button, email the wishlist to a friend (configurable), and share via a unique wishlist URL. Store administrators can configure maximum wishlist items, whether to show product images, allow out-of-stock items, and whether items move or copy from wishlist to cart. The 'Add to wishlist' button appears on product details pages and can be disabled per product.

The system shall provide a full-featured shopping cart where users can add, update, and remove products before checkout. The shopping cart page displays product details including SKU, product image, product name, unit price, quantity (editable), total price, and a remove checkbox/button. Key cart features include: (1) Update Shopping Cart button to recalculate after quantity changes, (2) Continue Shopping button to return to catalog browsing, (3) Estimate Shipping popup to calculate shipping costs by entering country/state/zip, (4) Discount code box for entering coupon codes, (5) Gift card code box for redeeming gift cards, (6) Gift wrapping dropdown (checkout attribute), (7) Order totals section showing sub-total, shipping cost, tax, reward points earned, and total amount, (8) Terms of service checkbox (configurable), (9) Checkout button. The system supports mini-shopping cart dropdown on hover, configurable maximum cart items, product images in cart, cross-sell product display, and cart sharing between stores. Administrators can view all customer shopping carts via Sales → Shopping carts and wishlists.

The system shall allow users to search for products using a search bar with autocomplete suggestions. The search functionality is configurable via Configuration → Settings → Catalog settings. When Search autocomplete is enabled, a dropdown list appears as the user types, showing matching product names and optionally product images. The number of autocomplete results displayed is configurable (e.g., show top 10 matches). A 'Show link to all search results' option can be enabled when found items exceed the displayed autocomplete count. The minimum search term length is configurable (e.g., minimum 3 characters). Search supports filtering by category name, manufacturer name, price range, and keyword matching against product names, short descriptions, full descriptions, SKUs, and product tags. The search results page supports configurable page sizes and price range filtering.

The system shall display main product categories (e.g., Computers, Electronics, Apparel, Digital Downloads, Books, Jewelry, Gift Cards) for easy navigation. Categories are managed via Catalog → Categories and support hierarchical parent-child relationships (subcategories). Each category has a name, description, image, SEO-friendly URL, and display order. Categories can be configured to: appear in the top menu (Include in top menu checkbox), display on the home page (Show on home page checkbox), support page size selection by customers, enable price range filtering, and allow specification attribute filtering. Categories support breadcrumb navigation, configurable view modes (grid/list), product sorting options (by position, name, price, creation date), and ACL-based visibility restrictions per customer role. Categories can also be limited to specific stores in a multi-store configuration.

The system shall display promotional banners on the homepage to highlight featured products and offers. nopCommerce supports homepage promotional content through configurable widgets and the Nivo Slider plugin (or similar carousel/banner plugins). Banners can showcase featured products, seasonal promotions, new arrivals, or special offers. The homepage layout supports multiple content zones including category displays, featured products marked with 'Show on home page', bestseller sections, and news items. Promotional banners typically include eye-catching images, overlay text, and call-to-action buttons. The banner display order, visibility, and content are managed through the admin area under Content management or widget configuration. Store owners can schedule banners with start and end dates.

The system shall display product images, descriptions, and prices in categorized sections on the homepage. Products can be featured on the homepage by enabling the 'Show on home page' checkbox on the product edit page, with configurable display order (1 = top of list). The homepage displays product boxes containing: product image (with support for multiple images and thumbnails), product name as a clickable link, short description, price (including 'From' price display for variable products), old price for comparison (strikethrough), product rating stars, and 'Add to cart' / 'Add to wishlist' buttons. The catalog pages panel in settings allows configuring default view mode (grid or list), product image display, and whether to show short descriptions. Bestseller products can also be displayed on the home page with a configurable count.

The system shall provide a 'Learn More' button on promotional banners that redirects users to detailed product pages. When customers click the 'Learn More' or call-to-action button on a homepage banner, they are navigated to the corresponding product details page, category page, or promotional landing page. The product details page displays comprehensive information including: product name, full-size images with gallery/zoom functionality, short and full descriptions, SKU, manufacturer part number, GTIN, price (with old price comparison), availability status, specification attributes table, product attributes (color, size selectors), quantity input, 'Add to cart' button, 'Add to wishlist' button, 'Add to compare list' button, 'Email a friend' button, product tags, product reviews, related products, and cross-sell products. The destination URL is configurable per banner through the widget/slider management interface.

The system shall provide a documentation link for users to access guides and FAQs about the platform. nopCommerce provides built-in support for informational content pages through the Topics (pages) feature (Content management → Topics). Topics are free-form content blocks used for FAQ pages, policy pages, terms of service, 'About Us', privacy policy, shipping information, and other informational content. Each topic supports a rich-text editor for content, can be included in the top menu, footer columns (1, 2, or 3), or sitemap, and supports SEO configuration (meta title, keywords, description, friendly URL). Topics can be password-protected, limited to specific customer roles or stores, and accessible when the store is closed. The documentation link in the footer directs users to the official nopCommerce documentation at docs.nopcommerce.com or to a custom store FAQ page.

The system shall provide a link to the nopCommerce forums where users can engage in discussions and seek support. nopCommerce includes a built-in forum system (Content management → Forums) that supports forum groups containing multiple forums, threaded topic discussions, and post replies. Forum features include: configurable permissions for guests and registered users (create topics, create posts, edit/delete posts), private messaging between users, forum subscriptions and email notifications, post voting, signature support, BBCode or simple text editor, RSS feeds per forum and for active discussions, and configurable page sizes for topics, posts, and search results. Forums can be enabled/disabled via Configuration → Settings → Forum settings. The forum link appears in the site navigation when forums are enabled.

The system shall support a product carousel feature to showcase multiple promotional items dynamically. nopCommerce supports dynamic product carousels through built-in widgets and third-party plugins (such as Nivo Slider). Carousels can display featured products, new arrivals, bestsellers, or promotional items with automatic rotation/sliding. Each carousel item typically includes a product image, name, price, and a clickable link to the product details page. The 'Recently viewed products' feature (configurable in Catalog settings → Additional sections) also provides a dynamic display of products the customer has browsed. The number of products displayed, rotation speed, and display order are configurable through the widget settings. Homepage product sections like 'Show on home page' products and bestsellers can also be displayed in carousel format depending on the theme.

The system shall provide a contact link or customer support access for user inquiries and issues. nopCommerce includes a built-in Contact Us page (typically accessible via the footer or 'Contact us' link) with a contact form that collects: Full Name, Email address, and Enquiry message (all configurable as required fields). When a user submits the form, an email notification is sent to the store's designated support email address using the configured email account. The system uses the 'ContactUs.EmailAMessage' message template for formatting the notification. Vendors can also have individual contact forms if 'Allow customers to contact vendors' is enabled in Vendor settings. CAPTCHA can be enabled on the contact form for anti-spam protection. Additionally, stores can integrate live chat plugins from the nopCommerce marketplace for real-time customer support.

The system shall allow users to search for products using a keyword input field. The search box is displayed in the header of the public store and accepts keyword input. When a user enters a search term and submits, the system searches across product names, short descriptions, full descriptions, product tags, and SKUs. The search results page displays matching products with images, names, prices, ratings, and 'Add to cart' buttons. The minimum search term length is configurable (Catalog settings → Search term minimum length). Search supports keyword highlighting and relevance-based ordering. The search can also be configured to search within manufacturer names and category names. Popular search keywords are tracked and displayed on the admin dashboard for analytics.

The system shall provide an 'Advanced Search' option to refine search results based on additional criteria. The Advanced Search page allows customers to filter products using multiple criteria: keyword input, Search in product descriptions checkbox, category selection (with 'Automatically search sub categories' option), manufacturer selection, price range (from/to), and optionally search within a specific store in multi-store setups. When the 'Allow customers to search with manufacturer name' and 'Allow customers to search with category name' settings are enabled in Catalog settings, the search engine includes these fields in the matching. Advanced search results support the same display options as standard search: configurable page sizes, grid/list view toggle, sorting options, and price range filtering.

The system shall display search results with product images, names, and prices. Search results are presented in a product listing format showing: product image (primary image from the product's multimedia gallery), product name as a clickable link to the product details page, short description (if enabled via catalog settings), price (including 'From' prices for variable products and old price strikethrough for discounted items), product rating stars (based on approved customer reviews), and action buttons ('Add to cart', 'Add to wishlist', 'Add to compare list'). The results support both grid and list view modes, with the default mode configurable in Catalog settings. Each product box follows the same template as category and manufacturer product listings.

The system shall allow users to add a product to the shopping cart from the search results page. Each product displayed in search results includes an 'Add to cart' button. For simple products without required attributes, clicking the button adds the product directly to the cart with a default quantity of 1. For products with required attributes (e.g., size, color), the user is redirected to the product details page to select attribute values before adding to cart. After adding, the system can either display the cart page immediately or keep the user on the current page (configurable via 'Display cart after adding a product' in Shopping cart settings). The mini-shopping cart dropdown updates to reflect the newly added item. The system validates stock availability, minimum/maximum cart quantities, and allowed quantity restrictions before adding.

The system shall allow users to sort search results based on criteria such as price, relevance, and ratings. Product sorting is configurable via Configuration → Settings → Catalog settings → Product sorting panel. Available sorting options include: Position (default/relevance), Name (A-Z, Z-A), Price (Low to High, High to Low), and Date of creation (newest first). Each sorting option can be individually activated or deactivated and assigned a display order by the store administrator. The 'Allow product sorting' checkbox enables or disables the sorting dropdown on category, manufacturer, and search result pages. The sorting dropdown appears at the top of the product listing and applies immediately when a selection is made. AJAX-based product loading can be enabled for smoother sorting without full page reloads.

The system shall allow users to choose the number of results displayed per page (e.g., 6, 12, etc.). Page size configuration is available for search results, categories, and manufacturers. When 'Allow customers to select page size' is enabled (in Catalog settings or per-category/manufacturer), a page size dropdown appears on the listing page. The available options are defined as a comma-separated list in 'Page size options' (e.g., '6, 3, 9'). The first value in the list is used as the default page size. When the setting is disabled, a fixed 'Page size' value is used instead. Pagination controls appear at the bottom of the product listing showing page numbers and navigation arrows. AJAX-based product loading can be enabled (via 'Use AJAX products loading' setting) to load pages without full page refreshes.

The system shall display product ratings based on customer reviews. Product ratings are calculated from approved customer reviews and displayed as star ratings (typically 1-5 stars) on product boxes in category pages, search results, and the product details page. In nopCommerce, product reviews can be configured to require store owner approval before appearing (Catalog settings → 'Product reviews must be approved'). Review settings include: one review per product per customer, allow anonymous reviews, require product purchase before reviewing, notify store owner of new reviews, and configurable reviews per page. Additionally, custom review types can be created (e.g., Quality, Value, Price ratings) to allow multi-dimensional rating. The product details page shows individual review entries with title, text, rating stars, reviewer name, date, and helpfulness voting (Yes/No).

The system shall provide a sidebar listing product categories for quick navigation. The category navigation block is displayed in the sidebar (left column) of the public store, showing a hierarchical tree of product categories. Categories are displayed based on their configured display order, with subcategories nested under parent categories. The sidebar can show the number of products in each category (configurable via 'Show number of distinct products beside each category' in Catalog settings). Clicking a category navigates to the category details page showing all products in that category. The Include in top menu setting additionally places popular categories in the top navigation bar. Category filtering supports specification attributes, price ranges, and manufacturer filters on the category page.

The system shall allow users to filter search results by manufacturer (e.g., Apple, HP). nopCommerce supports manufacturer filtering on catalog pages when 'Enable manufacturer filtering' is enabled in Catalog settings. Manufacturers are managed via Catalog → Manufacturers and each manufacturer has a name, description, image, SEO-friendly URL, display order, page size options, and price range filtering. Products are assigned to manufacturers on the product edit page. The manufacturer filter appears on category and search result pages as a sidebar filter or dropdown. The manufacturer navigation block displays in the sidebar with a configurable number of manufacturers to show. Manufacturer details pages list all products by that manufacturer with sorting, view mode toggling, and pagination. Manufacturers can be limited to specific stores and customer roles.

The system shall provide a 'Popular Tags' section to help users discover products based on trending keywords. Product tags are keywords assigned to products for identification and narrow search (managed via Catalog → Product tags). Tags are entered as comma-separated values on the product edit page. The Popular Tags cloud is displayed on the sidebar of the catalog page, where the size of each tag is proportional to the number of products associated with it — more products means a larger tag in the cloud. The number of tags displayed in the cloud is configurable via Catalog settings → Tags → 'Number of product tags (cloud)'. Clicking a tag shows all products associated with that tag on a 'Products by tag' page, which supports configurable page sizes, price range filtering, and sorting. Tags are SEO-friendly and can drive organic search traffic.

The system shall allow users to select a preferred currency for price display. This requirement is functionally identical to REQ001. The currency selection dropdown is accessible from the header/footer area and allows customers to switch between configured currencies. All prices throughout the store — including product prices, shipping costs, taxes, discounts, gift card values, and order totals — are dynamically converted using the exchange rate defined for each currency in Configuration → Currencies. The primary store currency is used for all internal calculations and payment processing. The system supports automatic and manual exchange rate updates. Currency display format (symbol, decimal places, position) follows the configured culture settings.

The system shall provide options to switch between grid and list views for search results. The view mode toggle is available on category pages, manufacturer pages, and search result pages when 'Allow view mode changing' is enabled in Catalog settings → Catalog pages. The default view mode (Grid or List) is configurable. In Grid view, products are displayed in a multi-column card layout with product image, name, price, rating, and action buttons. In List view, products are displayed in a single-column layout with larger images and more detailed information including short descriptions. The selected view mode persists across page navigation within the session. The view mode toggle appears as icon buttons (grid icon / list icon) near the top of the product listing alongside sorting and page size controls.

The system shall provide access to user authentication features, including login and registration, from the header. The header of the public store displays authentication links: 'Register' and 'Log in' for anonymous visitors, and 'My account' and 'Log out' for authenticated users. The header also displays the shopping cart icon with item count, wishlist link with item count, and optionally the customer's name. Registration and login links navigate to their respective pages. The 'My account' link provides access to customer information, addresses, orders, downloadable products, back-in-stock subscriptions, reward points, change password, and other account management features. The header layout is part of the store theme and can be customized through theme templates.

The system shall allow logged-in users to add products to a wishlist from the search results. Each product listing in search results, category pages, and manufacturer pages includes an 'Add to wishlist' button (unless disabled per product via the 'Disable wishlist button' setting). Clicking the button adds the product to the user's wishlist with a default quantity of 1. For products with required attributes, the user is redirected to the product details page first. The system can either display the wishlist page immediately after adding (configurable via 'Display wishlist after adding a product') or keep the user on the current page. The wishlist icon in the header updates to reflect the new count. Guest users are prompted to log in or register before accessing wishlist functionality. Maximum wishlist items are configurable per store.

The system shall display detailed product information including name, image, description, specifications, and price. The product details page presents comprehensive product information: product name (H1 heading), image gallery with multiple pictures (supporting alt text, title, and display order) and optional video embeds from YouTube/Vimeo, short description, full description (rich text with HTML support), SKU, manufacturer part number, GTIN, price (with old price comparison, tax info, and PAngV base price for German compliance), availability status, delivery date, specification attributes table (filterable attributes like screen size, CPU type displayed in a structured table), product attributes (dropdowns, radio buttons, checkboxes, color squares for variations like size/color with price/weight adjustments), product tags, breadcrumb navigation, share buttons (AddThis integration), and 'Email a friend' option. The page also shows related products and cross-sell items.

The system shall allow users to add the selected product to the shopping cart. The product details page features a prominent 'Add to cart' button. Before adding, the user can select product attributes (size, color, etc.), specify quantity, and choose a shipping address (if applicable). The system validates: required attribute selections, stock availability (if inventory tracking is enabled), minimum/maximum cart quantities, allowed quantity restrictions, and existing attribute combination availability. For pre-order products, the button displays as 'Pre-order' instead. For 'Call for price' products, a 'Call for pricing' message replaces the button. The system supports configurable behavior after adding: redirect to cart page or remain on product page. The mini-shopping cart in the header updates with the new item count and details.

The system shall allow users to specify the quantity of the product before adding it to the cart. The product details page includes a quantity input field (text box or dropdown) next to the 'Add to cart' button. By default, a numeric text box is displayed allowing the user to enter any quantity. However, if the store administrator has configured 'Allowed quantities' for the product (a comma-separated list like '1, 3, 5, 10'), the text box is replaced with a dropdown list of those specific quantities. The system enforces minimum cart quantity and maximum cart quantity restrictions configured per product. If inventory tracking is enabled, the system validates that the requested quantity does not exceed available stock (unless backorders are allowed). For products tracked by attribute combinations, stock validation applies per combination.

The system shall provide an option for logged-in users to add the product to their wishlist. The product details page includes an 'Add to wishlist' button below or beside the 'Add to cart' button. This button is visible unless the 'Disable wishlist button' checkbox is selected for the product. When clicked, the product (with any selected attribute values and specified quantity) is added to the customer's wishlist. The system can be configured to display the wishlist page immediately after adding or keep the customer on the product page. If a product is already in the wishlist, adding it again increments the quantity. The wishlist supports a configurable maximum number of items. Out-of-stock items can optionally be added to the wishlist based on store configuration.

The system shall allow users to add the product to a comparison list. The 'Compare products' feature is enabled via Catalog settings → 'Compare products' enabled checkbox. When enabled, an 'Add to compare list' button appears on product details pages and product boxes in listings. Customers can add multiple products to compare their specification attributes, prices, and descriptions side by side on a dedicated comparison page. The comparison page displays: product images, names, prices, short descriptions (if 'Include short description in compare products' is enabled), full descriptions (if 'Include full description in compare products' is enabled), and all specification attributes in a tabular format for easy side-by-side comparison. Users can remove products from the comparison list individually.

The system shall allow users to share product details via email. The 'Email a friend' feature is enabled via Catalog settings → 'Email a friend' enabled checkbox. When enabled, a 'Email a friend' button/link appears on the product details page. Clicking it opens a form where the user enters: friend's email address, their own email (auto-populated if logged in), and a personal message. The system uses the 'Product.EmailAFriend' message template to format and send the email containing the product name, description, image, price, and a link to the product page. An option to 'Allow anonymous users to email a friend' can be enabled or disabled. CAPTCHA can be configured for anti-spam protection on this form.

The system shall provide breadcrumb navigation to help users track their location within the site hierarchy. Breadcrumb navigation is displayed as a horizontal bar at the top of product pages, category pages, and other content pages, showing the hierarchical path from the home page to the current page. For example: Home > Computers > Desktops > Product Name. Each element in the breadcrumb is a clickable hyperlink navigating to the corresponding page. Breadcrumb navigation is enabled via Catalog settings → 'Category breadcrumb enabled' checkbox. For products in multiple categories, the breadcrumb displays the category path based on the referring category or the first assigned category. Breadcrumbs improve user navigation and contribute to SEO by providing structured internal linking.

The system shall display product ratings and reviews on the product details page. The product details page includes a reviews section showing: overall rating (star display), individual review entries with title, review text, star rating, reviewer name/date, and helpfulness voting (Was this review helpful? Yes/No). Customers can submit reviews by clicking 'Add your review' which opens a form with: review title, review text, and rating selection (1-5 stars). Custom review types (e.g., Quality, Value, Price) can be configured to collect multi-dimensional ratings. Reviews can be configured to require approval before display, limit to one review per product per customer, require product purchase, and allow replies from the store owner. Reviews are sortable by creation date.

The system shall allow only registered users to submit reviews. Product review submission can be restricted to registered (logged-in) users only by configuring review settings in Catalog settings. When 'Allow anonymous users to write product reviews' is disabled, only authenticated users can submit reviews — anonymous visitors see a prompt to log in or register. Additionally, the 'Product review possible only after product purchasing' setting restricts reviews to customers who have actually ordered the product. Submitted reviews include a title, review text, and rating (1-5 stars plus any configured custom review types). The system notifies the store owner about new reviews (configurable) and can notify the customer when their review receives a reply. Reviews appear on the customer's 'My Account' page if enabled.

The system shall provide an option for users to select the address they want to ship to before adding the product to the cart. nopCommerce supports an 'Estimate shipping' feature that can be displayed on both the shopping cart page and the product details page (configurable via Configuration → Settings → Shipping settings → 'Estimate shipping enabled (product page)' and 'Estimate shipping enabled (cart page)'). On the product details page, a shipping estimation popup allows the customer to enter country, state/province, and zip/postal code to see available shipping options and costs before adding to cart. On the shopping cart page, the 'Estimate Shipping' button opens a similar popup. During checkout, the shipping address step allows customers to select from saved addresses or enter a new shipping address with full address details.

The system shall display the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for the product. The SKU is a unique internal identifier used for inventory tracking and is entered on the product edit page in the 'Product info' panel. SKU display is controlled by two settings in Catalog settings → Product fields: 'Show SKU on product details page' and 'Show SKU on catalog pages'. When enabled, the SKU appears on the product details page near the product name/price area and optionally on category/search listing pages. The SKU is also displayed in shopping cart line items, wishlist entries, order details, invoices, and shipment records. For products with attribute combinations, each combination can have its own unique SKU that replaces the base product SKU when that combination is selected.

The system shall display the shopping cart with product details including SKU, image, name, price, quantity, total price, and remove option. The shopping cart page presents a tabular layout with columns: Remove checkbox, product image (if 'Show product images on cart' is enabled), product name (clickable link to product page), SKU, unit price, quantity input field (editable), and line total (unit price × quantity). Below the product table, the cart displays: gift wrapping options (checkout attributes), discount code input with 'Apply coupon' button, gift card code input with 'Add gift card' button, and an order totals summary showing sub-total, shipping cost estimate, tax amount, reward points earned, and grand total. Action buttons include 'Update shopping cart', 'Continue shopping', 'Estimate shipping', and 'Checkout'. The cart supports configurable maximum items and product image display.

The system shall allow users to update the quantity of items in the shopping cart. Each product row in the shopping cart contains an editable quantity (Qty) field. The customer can change the numeric value and click the 'Update shopping cart' button to recalculate all line totals and the order summary. The system validates: minimum cart quantity, maximum cart quantity, allowed quantities (if restricted to specific values), and stock availability (if inventory tracking is enabled). If the entered quantity exceeds available stock and backorders are not allowed, an appropriate error message is displayed. Quantity updates also trigger recalculation of applicable discounts, tier prices, shipping estimates, and tax amounts. The mini-shopping cart in the header also updates to reflect the new quantities.

The system shall allow users to remove items from the shopping cart. Each product row in the shopping cart includes a 'Remove' checkbox (or button). The customer selects the checkbox for item(s) to remove and clicks the 'Update shopping cart' button, which removes the selected items and recalculates the order totals. Removing items updates: the cart item count in the header, the mini-shopping cart dropdown, applicable discount calculations (some discounts may no longer apply), shipping cost estimates, and tax amounts. If all items are removed, the cart displays an empty cart message with a 'Continue shopping' or similar link. For products that require other products, the system can optionally auto-remove the required products when the main product is removed (configurable via 'Remove required products' in Catalog settings).

The system shall provide an option to select gift wrapping for products in the shopping cart. Gift wrapping is implemented as a checkout attribute in nopCommerce (Catalog → Attributes → Checkout attributes). A checkout attribute for gift wrapping typically appears as a dropdown on the shopping cart page with options like 'No', 'Yes ($X.XX)'. The store administrator creates this attribute with control type (dropdown, radio buttons, checkboxes), optional price adjustments per value, weight adjustments, tax category, and display order. Checkout attributes can be conditional (appear only when certain conditions are met), required or optional, and limited to specific stores. The 'Shippable product required' option ensures the attribute only appears when the cart contains products requiring shipping. The gift wrapping cost is added to the order total.

The system shall allow users to apply discount codes to their order. The shopping cart page displays a 'Discount code' input box (visible when 'Show discount box' is enabled in Shopping cart settings). Customers enter a coupon code and click 'Apply coupon' to activate the discount. nopCommerce supports multiple discount types: Assigned to order total, Assigned to products, Assigned to categories, Assigned to manufacturers, Assigned to shipping, and Assigned to order subtotal. Discounts can be percentage-based or fixed amount, with optional maximum discount limits. Discount features include: coupon code requirement, start/end date scheduling, cumulative discount support, usage limitations (unlimited, N times only, N times per customer), maximum discounted quantity, and configurable discount requirements (e.g., must be assigned to a customer role, minimum order amount). Multiple coupon codes can be applied to a single order if they fit.

The system shall allow users to redeem gift cards by entering a gift card code. The shopping cart page displays a 'Gift card' input box (visible when 'Show gift card box' is enabled in Shopping cart settings). Customers enter the gift card coupon code and click 'Add gift card' to apply it to the order. Gift cards in nopCommerce can be Virtual (delivered via email) or Physical (shipped). Gift cards are created as products with the 'Is gift card' checkbox enabled. After purchase, gift cards are activated either automatically when the order is completed or manually by the administrator. Each gift card has a unique coupon code, initial value, remaining balance, recipient details, and usage history. Gift card values are deducted from the order total, and remaining balances carry over for future use. Customers can optionally check gift card balances (with CAPTCHA protection).

The system shall provide a 'Continue Shopping' button to allow users to return to product browsing. The 'Continue shopping' button is displayed on the shopping cart page, allowing customers to navigate back to the catalog to continue browsing products. When clicked, the button typically redirects the customer to the last visited category page, the homepage, or the catalog root. This enables a seamless shopping experience where customers can add items to the cart incrementally without being locked into the checkout flow. The button is always visible on the shopping cart page alongside the 'Checkout' button.

The system shall provide an 'Estimate Shipping' button to calculate potential shipping costs before checkout. The 'Estimate Shipping' button is displayed on the shopping cart page when 'Estimate shipping enabled (cart page)' is enabled in Configuration → Settings → Shipping settings. Clicking the button opens a popup/modal where the customer enters: Country (dropdown), State/Province (dropdown, populated based on country), and Zip/Postal code. The system calculates available shipping options (e.g., Ground, Next Day Air, 2nd Day Air) with their respective costs based on the cart contents, product weights/dimensions, and configured shipping methods/providers. Results display shipping method names, descriptions, and costs. The customer can select a preferred shipping option, which is then reflected in the order totals section. Shipping computation methods include fixed rates, by weight, by total, and real-time carrier rate calculation.

The system shall display an order summary including sub-total, shipping cost, tax, and total amount. The order totals section appears on the shopping cart page and throughout the checkout process. It displays: Sub-total (sum of all line item totals), Shipping cost (calculated based on selected shipping method, product weights, and destination), Tax (calculated based on tax settings, product tax categories, and customer address), any applied Discount amounts (with coupon code reference), Gift card deductions (if applied), Reward points earned for the order, and the Grand Total. During checkout, the order summary is updated at each step as the customer provides billing/shipping addresses and selects shipping/payment methods. The 'Order totals on payment info tab' option can display the summary on the payment step in one-page checkout. Tax display can be configured as inclusive or exclusive.

The system shall provide a 'Checkout' button for users to proceed to the checkout process. The 'Checkout' button is prominently displayed on the shopping cart page. nopCommerce supports two checkout modes: (1) Multi-step checkout — a wizard-style process with separate pages for billing address, shipping address, shipping method, payment method, payment information, and order confirmation. (2) One-page checkout — all checkout steps on a single page with collapsible sections (enabled via 'Use one page checkout' in Order settings). Before proceeding to checkout, the system validates: Terms of service agreement (if required), minimum order subtotal/total, cart item stock availability, and user authentication (login/register or guest checkout). The checkout process flow: Billing Address → Shipping Address (or 'Ship to same address') → Shipping Method → Payment Method → Payment Information → Confirm Order → Order Completed.

The system shall require users to agree to the terms of service before proceeding to checkout. Terms of service agreement is configurable in Configuration → Settings → Order settings. Two options are available: (1) 'Terms of service (shopping cart page)' — requires agreement on the shopping cart page before the checkout button becomes active, and (2) 'Terms of service (confirm order page)' — requires agreement on the order confirmation step. The terms of service content is managed through a Topic/Page (Content management → Topics) with the system name 'ConditionsOfUse'. The checkbox text includes a clickable link that opens the terms of service content in a popup or new window. The checkout process is blocked until the customer checks the agreement box.

The system shall allow users to proceed to checkout as a guest without requiring account registration. Guest checkout is enabled via 'Anonymous checkout allowed' in Configuration → Settings → Order settings. When enabled, the checkout login page presents three options: (1) Checkout as Guest — allows completing the purchase without creating an account, (2) Register — create a new account before checkout, and (3) Returning Customer — log in with existing credentials. Guest customers provide their billing and shipping information during checkout but do not create a persistent account. Guest checkout can be disabled for specific scenarios like downloadable products ('Require registration for downloadable products'). Note: some features like wishlists, order history, and reward points require a registered account. The billing address step for guests requires mandatory fields (name, email, address details).

The system shall provide a registration option for users to create an account for future convenience. The registration page (accessible via header 'Register' link or during checkout) includes configurable form fields: Gender selection (Male/Female), First name, Last name, Date of birth (with minimum age validation), Email address, Company name (optional), Newsletter subscription checkbox (with configurable default state), Password and Confirm password fields. Password validation enforces configurable complexity rules: minimum length, at least one uppercase letter, at least one lowercase letter, at least one digit, at least one special character, and unduplicated passwords. Registration methods include: Standard (immediate access), Email validation required (confirmation email sent), and Administrator approval required. CAPTCHA can be enabled on the registration form. The 'Accept privacy policy' checkbox can be required. After registration, the customer is assigned to the 'Registered' customer role.

The system shall provide a login form for returning customers to access their accounts. The login page includes: Email/Username input field (depending on whether usernames are enabled in Customer settings), Password input field, 'Remember me' checkbox, 'Forgot password?' link, and 'Log in' button. The system validates credentials against the database using the configured password format (Clear, Hashed, or Encrypted). Security features include: configurable maximum login failures before lockout, lockout duration, and password lifetime/expiration policies. Login supports external authentication methods (e.g., Facebook, Google) if configured. After successful login, the customer is redirected to their previous page or the account dashboard. The login form also appears during checkout for returning customers, alongside guest checkout and registration options.

The system shall provide a 'Remember Me' checkbox to allow users to stay logged in on future visits. The 'Remember me' checkbox is displayed on the login form. When selected, the authentication cookie is set with an extended expiration period, allowing the customer to remain authenticated across browser sessions without re-entering credentials. When not selected, the authentication cookie expires at the end of the browser session. The persistent login uses secure, encrypted authentication tokens stored as browser cookies. This feature respects the customer's session security preferences and can be configured at the application level.

The system shall provide a 'Forgot Password' link for users to reset their password if needed. The 'Forgot password?' link on the login page navigates to a password recovery page where the customer enters their registered email address. The system sends a password recovery email using the 'Customer.PasswordRecovery' message template containing a unique, time-limited reset link. The 'Password recovery link. Days valid' setting (in Customer settings → Password and security) defines the link's validity period (set to 0 for no expiration). After clicking the reset link, the customer is directed to a page where they enter and confirm a new password, subject to the same password complexity validation rules as registration. The system enforces unduplicated password rules if configured.

The system shall validate email and password fields to ensure correct user input. Email validation ensures the entered email follows a valid format (e.g., user@domain.com) using standard email regex patterns. The system can optionally require email entry twice during registration ('Force entering email twice'). Password validation enforces configurable complexity rules: minimum length, at least one lowercase character, at least one uppercase character, at least one non-alphanumeric character, at least one digit, and unduplicated password history. During login, the system validates that the email/username exists and the password matches the stored hash. All form validations display inline error messages highlighting the specific field with the validation failure. Client-side validation provides immediate feedback, while server-side validation ensures security.

The system shall implement security measures such as encryption and protection against brute-force attacks during login. nopCommerce implements multiple security measures: (1) Password storage — passwords are stored using configurable formats: Hashed (recommended, using secure hashing algorithms), Encrypted, or Clear text. (2) Brute-force protection — configurable 'Maximum login failures' threshold, after which the account is locked for a configurable 'Lockout time (login failures)' duration in minutes. (3) SSL/HTTPS support — the system supports SSL certificates for encrypted communication. (4) CAPTCHA — configurable reCAPTCHA on login, registration, contact forms, and other sensitive pages. (5) Password policies — password lifetime/expiration, complexity requirements, and unduplicated password history. (6) PCI DSS compliance guidance — for credit card handling with secure payment processing. (7) Content Security Policy (CSP) headers support. (8) Anti-forgery tokens on forms to prevent CSRF attacks.

The system shall display appropriate error messages when invalid credentials are entered. When a user enters incorrect login credentials (wrong email/username or password), the system displays a clear error message such as 'Login was unsuccessful. Please correct the errors and try again. No customer account found' or 'The credentials provided are incorrect.' Error messages are designed to be informative without revealing whether the email exists in the system (for security). After reaching the maximum login failures threshold, the system displays a lockout message indicating the account is temporarily locked. Validation error messages also appear for: empty required fields, invalid email format, password not meeting complexity requirements, and account not activated (pending email verification or admin approval).

The system shall provide a button to allow users to continue to checkout as a guest. On the checkout login page, a 'Checkout as Guest' button is prominently displayed when anonymous checkout is allowed (Order settings → 'Anonymous checkout allowed'). Clicking the button bypasses the registration/login requirement and proceeds directly to the billing address step of the checkout process. Guest users must provide all required billing information including: first name, last name, email, country, state/province, city, address, zip/postal code, and phone number. The guest checkout option is not available if all products in the cart are downloadable and 'Require registration for downloadable products' is enabled in Customer settings.

The system shall provide a button to allow new users to register for an account. On the checkout login page, a 'Register' button navigates to the registration form. The registration form collects: Gender (if enabled), First name, Last name, Date of birth (if enabled, with minimum age validation), Email address, Company name (if enabled), Newsletter subscription checkbox, Password, and Confirm password. All enabled fields can be individually configured as required or optional in Customer settings → Customer form fields. After successful registration, the customer is either immediately logged in (standard registration), prompted to verify their email (email validation), or informed that admin approval is pending. The 'Register' button is also available from the header navigation for non-checkout registration.

The system shall provide a button to allow returning users to log in. On the checkout login page, a 'Log in' section provides email/username and password fields with a 'Log in' button. This allows returning customers to authenticate before proceeding to checkout, enabling access to saved addresses, order history, reward points, and other account-specific features during the checkout process. The login section includes the 'Forgot password?' link and 'Remember me' checkbox. After successful authentication, the customer proceeds to the billing address step with any previously saved addresses pre-populated for selection.

The system shall provide a form for users to enter their billing address details including first name, last name, email, company, country, state, city, address, postal code, phone number, and fax number. During the billing address step of checkout, the system presents an address form with all configurable fields. For registered customers, previously saved addresses are displayed for quick selection, with an option to enter a new address. Address form fields are configurable in Customer settings → Address form fields, where each field (Company, Street address, Street address 2, Zip/postal code, City, County/region, Country, State/province, Phone, Fax) can be individually enabled/disabled and set as required/optional. Country selection populates a dynamic State/Province dropdown. Custom address attributes can be added for additional fields beyond the defaults. The form supports international address formats.

The system shall enforce mandatory fields (marked with an asterisk) to ensure essential billing information is provided before proceeding. All required address form fields are marked with a red asterisk (*) indicator. The system prevents the customer from proceeding to the next checkout step until all mandatory fields are filled with valid data. Required fields are configurable per field in Customer settings → Address form fields (e.g., 'Street address' enabled + required, 'Phone number' enabled + required). Default mandatory fields typically include: First name, Last name, Email, Country, City, Address 1, and Zip/postal code. Both client-side (JavaScript) and server-side validation are performed. Error messages are displayed inline next to the specific field that failed validation, clearly indicating what information is missing or invalid.

The system shall provide dropdown menus for selecting country and state to ensure proper address format. The Country dropdown is populated from the country list managed in Configuration → Countries. The State/Province dropdown dynamically loads states/provinces based on the selected country via AJAX. Countries and states can be individually enabled/disabled, and the list includes international countries with ISO codes. The country selection also affects: available shipping methods and rates, tax calculations, address format validation (e.g., zip code format), and available payment methods. Some countries may not have states configured, in which case the state dropdown may show 'Other' or be hidden. The system supports country-specific address formatting rules.

The system shall provide an option for users to use the same billing address for shipping. During checkout, the billing address step includes a 'Ship to the same address' checkbox (enabled by default). When checked, the shipping address step is skipped, and the billing address is automatically used as the shipping address. When unchecked, the customer proceeds to a separate shipping address step where they can select from saved addresses or enter a new shipping address. The 'Disable Billing address step' option in Order settings can pre-fill billing information from the customer's default registration address. For products that don't require shipping (e.g., digital downloads, services), the shipping address step is automatically skipped.

The system shall provide a 'Continue' button to proceed to the next step in the checkout process. Each step in the multi-step checkout process includes a 'Continue' button at the bottom. The button is enabled only after all required fields are completed and validated. In multi-step checkout, clicking 'Continue' saves the current step's data and advances to the next step (e.g., Billing Address → Shipping Address → Shipping Method → Payment Method → Payment Info → Confirm Order). In one-page checkout, clicking 'Continue' on a section collapses it (showing a summary) and expands the next section. The button text may vary by step (e.g., 'Continue' or 'Confirm'). AJAX is used for seamless transitions between steps without full page reloads in one-page checkout mode.

The system shall validate all entered information to ensure completeness and correct formatting before allowing the user to continue. Comprehensive form validation is applied at each checkout step: (1) Billing/Shipping Address — validates required fields, email format, zip code format based on country, and phone number format if validation is enabled. (2) Shipping Method — ensures a shipping method is selected. (3) Payment Method — ensures a payment method is selected. (4) Payment Information — validates credit card number (Luhn algorithm), expiration date (not expired), CVV format, and cardholder name. Both client-side (JavaScript/jQuery validation) and server-side validation are performed. Phone number validation supports regex patterns (configurable in Customer settings). Error messages appear inline next to the relevant field with clear descriptions of the validation failure.

The system shall verify that the email address entered follows a valid email format. Email format validation is applied on all email input fields throughout the system (registration, checkout, contact forms, email-a-friend, newsletter subscription). The validation uses standard email format regex patterns to verify the structure: local-part@domain (e.g., user@example.com). Both client-side validation (immediate feedback as the user types or on form submission) and server-side validation (to prevent bypassing) are applied. If the format is invalid, an inline error message is displayed (e.g., 'Please enter a valid email address'). The 'Force entering email twice' setting in Customer settings can require customers to re-enter their email during registration for confirmation. Email uniqueness is also validated during registration.

The system shall validate the zip/postal code based on the selected country. The zip/postal code field is validated based on the customer's selected country to ensure it follows the correct format (e.g., 5-digit or ZIP+4 format for US, alphanumeric format for UK/Canada). The 'Zip/postal code' field can be enabled/disabled and set as required in Customer settings → Address form fields and Customer form fields. Validation rules may use regex patterns specific to each country's postal code format. The system displays an error message if the entered zip code does not match the expected format for the selected country. This validation applies to both billing and shipping address forms during checkout and in the customer account address management.

The system shall support international addresses by allowing users to select their country and input the appropriate details. nopCommerce supports international addresses through: (1) A comprehensive country list (Configuration → Countries) with ISO country codes, two-letter and three-letter codes, numeric codes, and configurable enabled/published states. (2) Dynamic state/province lists per country. (3) Configurable address form fields that accommodate different international address formats. (4) Multi-currency support for displaying prices in local currencies. (5) Multi-language support for the entire storefront. (6) International shipping methods and rate calculations based on destination country. (7) Tax configuration per country/state for VAT and other regional taxes. (8) Country-specific store preconfiguration during initial setup (language packs, VAT settings, currencies, measures, shipping details).

The system shall allow users to select a shipping method from available options (e.g., Ground, Next Day Air, 2nd Day Air). The shipping method step in checkout displays all available shipping options based on the customer's shipping address, cart contents (weight, dimensions), and configured shipping providers. nopCommerce supports multiple shipping computation methods: Fixed Rate Shipping, Shipping by Weight, Shipping by Total, and real-time carrier rate plugins (UPS, USPS, FedEx, etc.). Each shipping option displays: method name (e.g., 'Ground', 'Next Day Air', '2nd Day Air'), brief description, and shipping cost. Pickup in store can also be displayed on this step (configurable). The system also supports free shipping for specific products, customer roles, or orders meeting minimum thresholds. Shipping restrictions can limit methods based on country or customer role.

The system shall provide a brief description of each shipping method to inform users about the delivery type. Each shipping method displayed during checkout includes a name and a brief description explaining the service level (e.g., 'Ground — Delivery in 5-7 business days', 'Next Day Air — Overnight delivery by noon'). Descriptions are configured when setting up shipping methods in Configuration → Shipping → Shipping providers and their rate computation methods. For real-time carrier rates, descriptions are typically provided by the carrier's API response. The description helps customers make informed decisions about delivery speed vs. cost tradeoffs.

The system shall pre-select a default shipping method for user convenience. The shipping method step in checkout pre-selects the first available shipping option by default using a radio button selection. This ensures that the customer can quickly proceed to the next step without manual selection if the default option is acceptable. The pre-selected method is typically the first shipping option in the list, ordered by the shipping provider's configured display order or by cost (lowest first). Customers can change the selection by clicking a different radio button before clicking 'Continue'. The pre-selected shipping cost is reflected in the order totals summary.

The system shall provide a 'Back' button to allow users to return to the previous step in the checkout process. Each step in the multi-step checkout process (except the first step) includes a 'Back' button alongside the 'Continue' button. Clicking 'Back' navigates the customer to the previous checkout step while preserving all previously entered data (billing address, shipping address, etc.). In one-page checkout, the 'Back' functionality allows expanding a previous section to make changes. This enables customers to review and modify their selections (address, shipping method, payment method) at any point before confirming the order without losing their progress.

The system shall provide a 'Continue' button to allow users to proceed to the next step in the checkout process. This requirement applies to the shipping method selection step specifically. After the customer selects a shipping method (or accepts the pre-selected default), clicking the 'Continue' button validates the selection and advances to the payment method step. The button is active as long as a shipping method is selected (radio button checked). The order totals update to reflect the selected shipping cost before proceeding.

The system shall display the cost of each shipping method (even if $0.00) to ensure transparency in pricing. Every shipping option displayed during checkout shows its associated cost in the store's configured currency, including methods with zero cost (displayed as '$0.00' or 'Free'). This ensures complete pricing transparency so customers know exactly what they will pay for shipping. For products marked with 'Free shipping' on the product edit page, the shipping cost reflects this. The system also supports 'Additional shipping charge' per product, which is added on top of the base shipping rate. All shipping costs are included in the order totals summary, making the total cost clear before order confirmation.

The system shall ensure that a shipping method is selected before allowing users to continue to the next step. Validation on the shipping method step ensures that at least one shipping method radio button is selected before the 'Continue' button processes the request. If no shipping method is selected (though a default is typically pre-selected), the system displays a validation error message prompting the customer to select a shipping method. This server-side validation prevents proceeding to the payment step without a valid shipping selection. If no shipping methods are available for the customer's address (e.g., restricted shipping zones), an appropriate message is displayed.

The system shall allow users to select a payment method from available options (e.g., Check/Money Order, Credit Card). The payment method step in checkout displays all configured and active payment methods as radio button options. nopCommerce supports multiple payment method plugins: Check/Money Order, Credit Card (Manual), Purchase Order, PayPal (Standard, Smart Payment Buttons), Stripe, Square, Authorize.Net, and many more via the plugin marketplace. Each payment method is configured in Configuration → Payment methods with display order, restricted countries, and minimum/maximum order amounts. Payment methods can support different transaction modes: Authorize only, Authorize and Capture, or direct payment. The available methods may vary based on the order total, customer role, and shipping country.

The system shall provide a brief description of each payment method to inform users about the payment process. Each payment method displayed during checkout includes a name and descriptive text explaining how the payment works (e.g., 'Check / Money Order — Pay by check or money order', 'Credit Card — Pay using Visa, Mastercard, Amex'). Descriptions are configured in the payment method plugin settings. Some payment methods may also display logos (e.g., credit card brand logos). The descriptions help customers understand the payment process, expected timelines, and any special instructions associated with each method.

The system shall pre-select a default payment method for user convenience. The payment method step pre-selects the first available payment method using a radio button, allowing customers to quickly proceed if the default is acceptable. The pre-selection follows the configured display order of payment methods. Customers can change the selection by clicking a different payment method radio button. The selected payment method determines what additional information is collected in the next step (e.g., credit card details for card payments, no additional info for check/money order).

The system shall provide a 'Back' button to allow users to return to the previous step (shipping method) in the checkout process. The payment method step includes a 'Back' button that navigates to the shipping method step while preserving all entered data. This allows customers to modify their shipping selection if needed. All previously entered billing address, shipping address, and shipping method selections are retained when navigating back.

The system shall provide a 'Continue' button to allow users to proceed to the next step (payment information) in the checkout process. After selecting a payment method, clicking 'Continue' advances to the payment information step where additional details are collected based on the selected method (e.g., credit card form for card payments). The button validates that a payment method is selected before proceeding.

The system shall ensure that a payment method is selected before allowing users to continue to the next step. Validation on the payment method step ensures a radio button is selected. If no payment method is selected (though a default is typically pre-selected), a validation error prompts the customer to make a selection. If no payment methods are available or configured, an appropriate error message is displayed. Server-side validation prevents proceeding without a valid selection.

The system shall allow users to select a credit card type from a dropdown menu. When the Credit Card (Manual) payment method is selected, the payment information step displays a form including a 'Credit card type' dropdown with options such as Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and Discover. The available card types are configured in the payment method plugin settings. The selected card type is validated against the entered card number prefix (e.g., Visa starts with 4, Mastercard with 5). This field is required for manual credit card processing.

The system shall provide an input field for users to enter the name on the credit card. The 'Cardholder name' text field on the credit card payment form requires the customer to enter the full name as printed on the credit card. This is a required field validated for non-empty input. The name is used for payment processing and order records. Client-side and server-side validation ensure the field is completed before form submission.

The system shall provide an input field for users to enter their credit card number. The 'Card number' text field accepts the full credit card number (typically 13-19 digits). The field includes client-side validation using the Luhn algorithm to verify the number's mathematical validity before submission. The input may include auto-formatting (spaces between groups of digits) for readability. The card number is validated against the selected card type (Visa, Mastercard, etc.) based on number prefix patterns. For PCI DSS compliance, the full card number should only be transmitted over HTTPS/SSL encrypted connections.

The system shall provide dropdown menus for users to select the expiration month and year of the credit card. Two dropdown fields are displayed: Expiration Month (01-12) and Expiration Year (current year through several years in the future). The system validates that the selected expiration date is not in the past (i.e., the card has not expired). Both dropdowns are required fields. The expiration date is used in conjunction with the card number and CVV for payment authorization.

The system shall provide an input field for users to enter the card's security code (CVV). The 'Card code' or 'CVV/CVC' text field accepts the 3 or 4 digit security code printed on the credit card (3 digits for Visa/Mastercard on the back, 4 digits for American Express on the front). The field is validated for numeric input and correct length based on the selected card type. This code provides an additional layer of security for card-not-present transactions. The CVV is never stored in the database per PCI DSS requirements.

The system shall provide a 'Back' button to allow users to return to the previous step (payment method selection) in the checkout process. The payment information step includes a 'Back' button that returns to the payment method step while preserving entered credit card data in the current session. This allows customers to change their payment method selection if needed.

The system shall provide a 'Continue' button to allow users to proceed to the order confirmation step. After entering all payment information (credit card details), clicking 'Continue' validates all fields and advances to the Confirm Order step where the customer reviews the complete order summary before final submission.

The system shall validate all credit card details before allowing users to proceed to the next step. Comprehensive validation is performed on all credit card fields: (1) Card type — must be selected from the dropdown. (2) Cardholder name — required, non-empty. (3) Card number — required, numeric, valid length, passes Luhn algorithm check, matches selected card type prefix. (4) Expiration date — required, month and year selected, not expired. (5) Card code (CVV) — required, numeric, correct length (3 or 4 digits based on card type). Both client-side (JavaScript) and server-side validation are performed. Validation errors are displayed inline next to the specific field. The payment gateway may also return additional validation errors during authorization.

The system shall ensure secure handling of credit card information using encryption and compliance with PCI DSS. nopCommerce supports PCI DSS compliance through: (1) SSL/TLS encryption — all payment pages must use HTTPS to encrypt data in transit. (2) No storage of full card numbers — by default, credit card details are not stored in the nopCommerce database (except when using the Manual Credit Card method, where encrypted storage is optional). (3) Payment gateway integration — most payment methods redirect to the payment provider's secure page (hosted payment page) or use tokenization, so card data never touches the merchant's server. (4) Anti-forgery tokens on all forms. (5) Secure password storage using hashing. (6) Configurable security headers (CSP). Store owners are advised to use a dedicated IP address with SSL certificate and ensure hosting meets PCI compliance requirements.

The system shall display details of the ordered products on the order confirmation page, including SKU, image, product name, price, quantity, and total cost. The Confirm Order step (final step before placing the order) displays a comprehensive order review including: (1) Billing address summary. (2) Shipping address summary. (3) Selected shipping method with cost. (4) Selected payment method. (5) Product table with columns: SKU, product image, product name (with selected attribute values), unit price, quantity, and line total. (6) Order totals: sub-total, shipping, tax, discounts, gift card deductions, and grand total. This allows the customer to review all details before clicking 'Confirm' to place the order.

The system shall calculate and display the order total including subtotal, shipping cost, tax, and final total amount. The order totals calculation includes: (1) Sub-total — sum of all product line totals (unit price × quantity, including attribute price adjustments and tier pricing). (2) Shipping — calculated based on selected method, product weights/dimensions, and destination. (3) Tax — calculated based on product tax categories, customer address, and tax provider settings (fixed rate or country/state-specific). (4) Discounts — any applicable discount amounts deducted. (5) Gift card — any applied gift card values deducted. (6) Reward points — if used as payment. (7) Grand Total — final amount to be charged. Minimum order subtotal and total amounts can be enforced (Order settings). The order total is displayed on the shopping cart page, throughout checkout, and on the order confirmation/completed pages.

The system shall provide a 'Back' button to allow users to return to the previous step (payment information) in the checkout process. The Confirm Order step includes a 'Back' button that returns to the payment information step, allowing the customer to modify payment details before finalizing the order. All previously entered checkout data (addresses, shipping, payment) is preserved.

The system shall validate that billing and shipping addresses are correctly displayed before order confirmation. The Confirm Order step displays the billing and shipping addresses as entered/selected during checkout. The system validates that both addresses contain all required fields and are properly formatted. Any address validation errors from previous steps would have been caught earlier, but the display allows the customer to visually verify accuracy. If the customer notices an error, they can use the 'Back' button to navigate to the address step and make corrections.

The system shall display the selected payment method for user verification before order confirmation. The Confirm Order step clearly displays the selected payment method name (e.g., 'Check / Money Order', 'Credit Card') and its current status. For credit card payments, a masked card number may be shown (e.g., '**** **** **** 1234'). This allows the customer to verify their payment selection before finalizing the order.

The system shall display the selected shipping method for user verification before order confirmation. The Confirm Order step displays the selected shipping method name (e.g., 'Ground', 'Next Day Air'), its description, and the associated shipping cost. This allows the customer to verify the delivery method and cost before placing the order. If the customer wants to change the shipping method, they can navigate back using the 'Back' button.

The system shall ensure that all order details are correct before allowing users to confirm the order. The Confirm Order step serves as the final verification checkpoint before order placement. It displays all order details: billing address, shipping address, shipping method with cost, payment method, product list with SKUs/images/names/prices/quantities/totals, and complete order totals (subtotal, shipping, tax, discounts, grand total). The 'Confirm' button submits the order only after all data passes final server-side validation including: address completeness, shipping method validity, payment method validity, product availability, stock verification, minimum order amounts, and terms of service agreement (if required at this step).

The system shall display a summary of the order after placement, including order number, order date, order status, and total order amount. After successful order placement, the Order Completed page (or Order Details page) displays: unique Order Number (with configurable format via Order ID counter and Order number mask in Order settings), Order Date (creation date/time), Order Status (Pending, Processing, Complete, or Cancelled), Payment Status (Pending, Authorized, Paid, etc.), Shipping Status (Not yet shipped, Shipped, Delivered, etc.), and the complete order total. Customers can also access their order history via My Account → Orders, where all past orders are listed with these summary details. The 'Disable Order completed page' option can automatically redirect to the order details page.

The system shall display the billing and shipping address details of the user on the order details page. The Order Details page (accessible via My Account → Orders → View) displays the full billing address (first name, last name, email, phone, fax, company, address lines, city, state/province, zip, country) and shipping address in separate sections. If the billing and shipping addresses are the same, both sections show the same information. For orders with 'Pick up in store' shipping, the pickup location details are displayed instead of a shipping address.

The system shall display the selected payment method and its current status on the order details page. The Order Details page shows the Payment Method name (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'Check / Money Order') and the Payment Status (e.g., 'Pending', 'Authorized', 'Paid', 'Refunded', 'Partially Refunded', 'Voided'). The payment status reflects the current state of the payment transaction and is updated as the order progresses through fulfillment.

The system shall display the selected shipping method and its current status on the order details page. The Order Details page shows the Shipping Method name (e.g., 'Ground', 'Next Day Air') and the Shipping Status (e.g., 'Not yet shipped', 'Partially shipped', 'Shipped', 'Delivered'). For shipped orders, tracking numbers and shipment details are available. The shipping status is updated as shipments are created and progressed by the store administrator.

The system shall display details of the purchased products on the order details page, including SKU, image, product name, price, quantity, and total cost. The Order Details page contains a Products section with a table showing: product SKU, product image, product name (as a clickable link to the product page, with any selected attribute values listed), unit price, quantity ordered, and line total. For downloadable products, download links are provided (if the order is paid and download is activated). The product list matches exactly what was ordered.

The system shall provide a 'Print' button to allow users to print their order details. The Order Details page includes a 'Print' button that opens a printer-friendly version of the order details in a new window/tab, optimized for printing. The printable view includes all order information: order number, date, status, billing/shipping addresses, payment method, shipping method, product table, and order totals. The layout removes navigation elements and uses a clean format suitable for paper printing.

The system shall provide a 'PDF Invoice' button to allow users to download their order invoice as a PDF. The Order Details page includes a 'PDF Invoice' button that generates and downloads a PDF invoice for the order. The PDF invoice includes: store logo, order number, order date, billing/shipping addresses, payment and shipping methods, product table with SKUs/names/prices/quantities/totals, and order totals summary. PDF invoice settings are configurable in Configuration → Settings → Order settings → PDF invoice panel. PDF invoices can also be automatically attached to order-related emails (order placed, order paid, order processing, order completed) based on configuration.

The system shall provide a 'Re-order' button to allow users to place the same order again.
